narration_text: Now we'll see how the internet is structured as a network of networks. Look at the diagram on your slide — notice how the access ISPs are connected to the global transit ISPs, forming a complex web of relationships. I want you to focus on the economic agreements between customer and provider ISPs, and how this arrangement enables data to flow across the globe. As you observe the image, you'll see that each access ISP doesn't need to connect to every other ISP, but rather can rely on the global transit ISPs to facilitate communication. Let's think about this together, and consider how this structure allows the internet to function efficiently, which will lead us to our next topic, exploring the implications of this network structure.
